action_items.action: Circulate FSS protection zone working paper and draft analysis of 7125 to 7250 MHz band segment
Compile FSS earth station inventory for the 7125 to 7250 MHz segment
Schedule next TIG meeting for the second week of January and send Doodle poll to members

claims.claim_text: The band under discussion is 7125 to 8400 MHz, with primary focus on the 7125 to 7250 MHz segment where FSS earth stations are concentrated
claims.speaker: LaSorte, Nickolas

claims.claim_text: The TIG is chartered to evaluate downlink sharing options between proposed mobile broadband and incumbent FSS receivers
claims.speaker: LaSorte, Nickolas

claims.claim_text: A single-point criterion of negative 10.5 dB at 0.03 percent would provide equivalent protection to the ITU two-point standard
claims.speaker: Bhatt, Sandip

claims.claim_text: DoW course of action number three proposes reallocation of the 7125 to 7250 MHz band segment
claims.speaker: DiFrancisco, Michael

claims.claim_text: Approximately 847 FSS earth stations are currently operating under primary status in the 7125 to 7250 MHz band segment
claims.speaker: DiFrancisco, Michael

decisions.decision_text: Meeting agenda approved: scope confirmation, sharing-criteria approach, and action items for the next session
decisions.rationale: Speaker explicitly states 'the agenda is approved' after asking for objections and hearing none. Direct commitment with explicit approval verb.

decisions.decision_text: Group approved application of ITU two-point criteria for FSS protection analysis: negative 10.5 dB at 80th percentile and negative 6 dB at 0.03 percent
decisions.rationale: Speaker explicitly states 'that is approved' after presenting the criteria and asking for objections with none heard. Direct commitment with explicit approval verb and precise numeric parameters.

decisions.decision_text: Decision on Course of Action (COA) selection deferred to the next working group meeting pending completion of working paper from Nick
decisions.rationale: Speaker explicitly uses 'defer' verb: 'We will defer the decision on COA selection to the next working group meeting.' Clear deferral with stated rationale (insufficient information) and condition (pending working paper).
